message:
feat(macros/session): generate transport-backed session constructors

Add gen_transport_constructor() to the session type proc macro that
generates a new_session_with_transport() function for each protocol
definition. This creates a matched initiator/responder pair backed by
mpsc channels wrapped in SessionTransport, enabling integration tests
and production code to construct transport-backed sessions without
manually wiring up channels.

The generated function:
- Takes a channel_id (for obligation tracking), optional obligation
  parameters, and buffer size
- Creates crossed mpsc channel pairs (initiator→responder, responder→
  initiator)
- Returns (Chan<Initiator, ...>, Chan<Responder, ...>) with
  SessionTransport wiring

The generated module now imports SessionTransport alongside the
existing session type machinery.

Adds macro-level test verifying the constructor is present in generated
code and uses Chan::new_with_transport.

Adds two integration tests exercising real protocol execution through
the generated constructors:
- send_permit_transport_constructor_is_available_and_works: exercises
  the full send-permit protocol (reserve → select commit → send payload
  → close) through transport-backed channels
- lease_transport_constructor_is_available_and_works: exercises the
  full lease protocol (acquire → select release → send release → close)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
